notes.anchor: go back to the beginnings of that friendship in our fathers
notes.note: Roman amicitia was a formal tie that could be inherited across generations; Bithynicus implies his own standing with Cicero is strong enough to rest on personal grounds alone, not requiring him to invoke the inherited obligation (hereditary paternal friendship) as a weaker suppliant might.

notes.anchor: in whatever matters there may be need
notes.note: The letter is written from Sicily (late March 44 BC, shortly after Caesar's assassination) and asks Cicero's protection in absentia; Bithynicus, whose father had been a Pompeian, was in a vulnerable position and needed a well-connected patron in Rome during the power vacuum.

notes.anchor: if you suppose that any service of yours toward me will not be allowed to die
notes.note: This conditional phrase is a formal Roman reciprocity formula: a promise that Cicero's officia will be remembered and returned, the standard currency of amicitia exchanges.
